channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: In Shortlands Rd, Shortlands about 20m SW of the traffic signal jct with Bromley Rd (opposite side idmf road from the station) both the pole-mounted litter bin and the freestanding boxy recycling bibs are full and overflowing rubbish onto the public footway., 18th June
item.description:
In Shortlands Rd, Shortlands about 20m SW of the traffic signal jct with Bromley Rd (opposite side idmf road from the station) both the pole-mounted litter bin and the freestanding boxy recycling bibs are full and overflowing rubbish onto the public footway. This has been like this for at least 3 days
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shortlands Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pelican crossing 'wait' does not display after pressing the button to cross. This is at the traffic signal to cross Shortlands Rd westbound, at the jct with Bromley Rd, on the railway station side of the road., 18th June
item.description:
Pelican crossing 'wait' does not display after pressing the button to cross. This is at the traffic signal to cross Shortlands Rd westbound, at the jct with Bromley Rd, on the railway station side of the road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shortlands Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The rubbish from your estate goes all over the pavement and road, 18th June
item.description:
Last time you laughingly claimed that you had solved this issue within one day, absolute BS. The bins are overflowing and all the rubbish ends up on the pavement and in the road. The incompetence of your residence and your caretakers is creating an environmental Hazard. It is literally like this every time the bins are due to be collected. Where is thin with sharp edges, use nappies and general rubbish. It is disgusting and I will take it further if you do not solve this issue. And no, cleaning up after it's already blown down the street is too late. I expect far too much from phoenix community housing to actually be competent at something.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravensbourne Avenue, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 5 inch hole in drain cover, 18th June
item.description:
A hole has appeared in a metal drain cover that could be a risk to children and animals using the woods

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bostall Hill, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish everywhere as usual, 18th June
item.description:
Always full of rubbish , never emptied , always piling on top and all over the floor

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bridge Gardens, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside jesmond dene hotel on the crossing, 18th June
item.description:
There is a huge gap in the stone which has been left to one side

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Chad's Street, King's Cross, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street lamp on busy main road not working. Dangerous, 17th June
item.description:
This street lamp (no. 20 on London Road) has not been turning on when it is dark for 2 weeks. This is extremely dangerous as cars drive very fast along this road (London Road) so this is a VERY BIG risk for accidents. Visibility is very low due to the faulty streetlamp. Please kindly fix this to avoid any accidents.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): London Road, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Western half of Achilles Road, and adjoining segments of Agamemnon Road, 17th June
item.description:
Street lighting for a large part of the block has gone off; western half of Achilles Road (20 or so main doors) and as far as I can see up Agamemnon Road (more than 20 main doors, including lots of flats).
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Agamemnon Road, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Stop line, pedestrians stud, zigzag faded on both approaches-required repainting, 17th June
item.description:
Stop line, pedestrians stud, zigzag faded on both approaches-required repainting
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kinloch Street, Holloway, Greater London

item.title: Stop line, pedestrians stud, zigzag faded on both approaches- required repainting, 17th June
item.description:
Stop line, pedestrians stud, zigzag faded on both approaches- required repainting
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 128, Junction Road, Archway, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The hedges/ bushes between 194 and 198 Brook st alley way is overgrown. Its not been maintained., 17th June
item.description:
The hedges / bushes in the alleyway along the property of 198 brook st has not been maintained. The overgrowth has climbed up the fence and into the garden, damaging parts of the property’s fence/ wall.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 199, Brook Street, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Yellow lines painted on odd numbers., 17th June
item.description:
Yellow lines painted on the odd side of the road are they going to be painted on the other side of the road also double yellow lines by the church still waiting to be extended . These yellow lines have been outstanding for a long time.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crescent Drive, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent! Manhole cover lifted and dangerous, 17th June
item.description:
This needs to be resolved as a matter of urgency

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parkside Square, Deptford, Greater London

item.title: Mattress dumped, 17th June
item.description:
Mattress dumped

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lullingstone Road, Upper Belvedere, Greater London

item.title: Fly tipping, 17th June
item.description:
Shelves dumped

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Middleton Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipped armchair blocking pavement outside number 23/25, 17th June
item.description:
A broken fabric armchair and detached wooden furniture panels have been dumped entirely on the public footpath directly outside number 23/25, creating a hazard for pedestrians.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 74, Lawrie Park Road, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: To the left if facing No. 2 Hartland Road, 17th June
item.description:
Tree roots have damaged pavement. Uneven paving stones which are now protruding at least 2.5cm. The whole area around the tree is completely uneven. This is an ongoing problem (at least 6 years) that has continued to worsen.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hartland Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Stop obstructions! to public footpaths!, 17th June
item.description:
Breach of public safety! highways! access blocked regularly! by long-term shop owners!! wealthy car owners!! endangering public safety! of all! including vunerable! elderly! frail! expectant mothers! mothers with buggies! strollers! wheel chair ♿️ disabled with cane! visually impaired! school.children!fine should be imposed! like car parking tickets!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lansdowne Way, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Granville Road, 17th June
item.description:
People constantly come here specifically to dump rubbish, because it is a dead-end street. Just now, a commercial Enterprise van dumped a wardrobe, adding to the piles of waste already here (including a washing machine and mess from people living in a caravan on-site). Please arrange for the removal of the current waste and consider this area for immediate CCTV monitoring.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Granville Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: Foliage blocking footpath, 17th June
item.description:
Bush between Colney Hatch Lane and Powerleague perimeter fence is almost blocking the entire footpath.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Colney Hatch Lane, Colney Hatch, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
